topic: A person learns their apartment building is going condo
raw_output:
[story 1]

Sunlight streamed through the window, painting stripes across the polished floorboards of Apartment 4B. Maya hummed a little tune as she rearranged a stack of brightly colored books on her shelf. "Oh, this is going to be so exciting!" she murmured to herself, tapping her chin with a wide smile. She had just received the official notice—the building was transitioning to condominiums. A bubbly feeling bubbled up inside her chest, making her bounce lightly on the balls of her feet. She immediately started sketching out ideas for decorating her new space, imagining vibrant colors and cozy corners. The prospect of owning her own slice of the building felt like a wonderful adventure waiting to unfold.

[story 2]

"Isn't this just the best news?" Liam exclaimed, practically skipping down the hallway, his shoulders practically vibrating with energy. He held the printed flyers, his eyes sparkling as he pointed towards the notice board. "We're getting condos! Imagine having our own little place!" A genuine grin stretched across his face, crinkling the corners of his eyes. He paced back and forth in his living room, a light, quick rhythm to his steps suggesting pure delight. He immediately started browsing online listings, his fingers flying across the keyboard with enthusiastic clicks. The future felt open, vast, and full of possibility.

[story 3]

Eleanor leaned back against the doorframe, a soft, pleased sigh escaping her lips. The news about the building becoming condos had settled over her like a warm blanket. She smoothed down her dress, her movements fluid and unhurried. "Well, this certainly changes things," she mused aloud, a bright, airy tone coloring her voice. She walked over to the balcony, taking a deep, satisfying breath of the afternoon air. A sense of buoyant anticipation settled over her; she couldn't wait to start planning her move, imagining the fresh start awaiting her in her own unit.
